title: Today's Tech Brief: How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment
summary: Pope Leo XIV’s new encyclical on artificial intelligence, Magnifica Humanitas, and OpenAI's biodefense program make headlines in tech.
imageAlt: Image from source article for How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment
imageCaption: How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment
imageSourceTitle: How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment

body.text: I'm looking at a fascinating piece from MIT Technology Review about Pope Leo XIV's new encyclical on artificial intelligence, dubbed Magnifica Humanitas. The document includes a striking statement: 'Technology is never neutral.' What stands out to me is the Pope's emphasis on the need for a more thoughtful approach to AI development. It's an interesting call to action, especially coming from a global spiritual leader. The Pope's words carry weight, and it's clear he's urging us to think carefully about how we create and use AI.

Axios is reporting that OpenAI has launched a biodefense program. My read is that this move is a significant expansion of their capabilities, potentially putting them at the forefront of AI applications in biotechnology. It's a development that's likely to raise eyebrows, given the sensitive nature of biodefense research. The intersection of AI and biodefense is a complex one, and I'm curious to see how this plays out.

How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Pope Leo XIV’s new encyclical on artificial intelligence includes a statement that warrants serious attention from technologists and policymakers: “Technology is never neutral.” Magnifica Humanitas (“Magnificent Humanity”) is a clarion call to all people to act with courage and solidarity as we enter an age already being transformed by artificial intelligen... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

Exclusive: OpenAI launches biodefense program - Axios, from Axios, is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Comprehensive up-to-date news coverage, aggregated from sources all over the world by Google News. Exclusive: OpenAI launches biodefense program Axios The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
So that is the brief version: a few headlines, a little context, and just enough connective tissue to make the day easier to follow without turning the whole thing into homework.
